title: BART
existing_short_definition: Facebook AI's encoder-decoder transformer pretrained as a denoising autoencoder, strong on summarization and other seq2seq tasks.

title: Base Model
existing_short_definition: A pretrained LLM that has not undergone instruction tuning or alignment — it completes text but does not follow chat-style instructions.

title: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)
existing_short_definition: Google's 2018 encoder-only transformer trained with masked language modeling — the foundation of most pre-2023 NLP pipelines.

title: BGE Embeddings
existing_short_definition: BAAI's BGE (BAAI General Embedding) family — strong open-weights text embedding models, now including multilingual and multi-functional variants.

title: BitNet (1.58-bit Models)
existing_short_definition: Microsoft's research line on LLMs with ternary weights ({-1, 0, +1}) trained with QAT, achieving near-parity with bf16 at a fraction of memory and inference cost.

title: Chat Model (Instruct Model)
existing_short_definition: A base model that has been post-trained (SFT + preference optimization) to follow instructions and hold conversations.

title: Claude (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Anthropic's closed LLM family — Haiku, Sonnet, Opus variants across Claude 2, 3, 3.5, and 4 — known for strong writing quality, safety training, and agentic capability.

title: CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's model that embeds images and text into a shared space via contrastive learning on 400M image-caption pairs — the backbone of most modern VLMs and image search.

title: CodeLlama
existing_short_definition: Meta's code specialization of Llama 2, fine-tuned on 500B+ code tokens with long-context and infilling support.

title: Command R (Cohere)
existing_short_definition: Cohere's open-weights LLM family (Command R and R+), tuned for enterprise use with strong RAG, tool use, and multilingual support.

title: Decoder
existing_short_definition: A model (or half) that generates tokens autoregressively, using causal attention so each token sees only previous tokens — the whole of GPT-family models.

title: DeepSeek (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: A Chinese lab's open-weights MoE and dense models — DeepSeek V2, V3, R1 — notable for strong reasoning and very low training cost.

title: DeepSeek Coder
existing_short_definition: DeepSeek's code-specialist LLM family with strong completion, infilling, and long-context performance on programming benchmarks.

title: DeepSeek R1
existing_short_definition: DeepSeek's open-weights reasoning model that achieved OpenAI o1-level performance using large-scale RL on a MoE base, released early 2025.

title: DETR (Detection Transformer)
existing_short_definition: An end-to-end object detector that replaces hand-crafted anchor boxes and NMS with a transformer decoder and bipartite matching.

title: Diffusion Model
existing_short_definition: A generative modeling approach that learns to reverse a gradual noising process, producing samples from pure noise in a series of denoising steps.

title: DINOv2 (Self-Supervised Vision)
existing_short_definition: Meta's self-supervised vision transformer that produces strong image features without any labels — the state-of-the-art self-supervised vision backbone.

title: Embedding Model
existing_short_definition: A model that maps text, images, or other inputs into fixed-size dense vectors where semantic similarity corresponds to vector proximity.

title: Encoder
existing_short_definition: A model (or model half) that transforms input tokens into contextualized representations — the bidirectional half of encoder-decoder and the whole of BERT-style models.

title: Encoder-Decoder
existing_short_definition: An architecture with a bidirectional encoder that reads the input and a causal decoder that generates the output, bridged by cross-attention.

title: Falcon (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: TII's (UAE) open-weights LLM family — Falcon 7B/40B/180B, then Falcon 2 and Falcon 3 — one of the early strong open alternatives to Llama.

title: Flux (Black Forest Labs)
existing_short_definition: Black Forest Labs' open-weights text-to-image diffusion transformer family — FLUX.1 [dev], [pro], [schnell] — pushing open image quality past SDXL.

title: Foundation Model
existing_short_definition: A large model trained on broad data that can be adapted to many downstream tasks — the base layer upon which instruction-tuned and task-specific models are built.

title: OpenAI o4 / Frontier Reasoning
existing_short_definition: The post-o1 reasoning-model lineage (o3, o4-mini, o4, and beyond) representing the cutting edge of LLM reasoning via RL on verifiable tasks and extended test-time compute.

title: Gemini (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Google DeepMind's closed multimodal LLM family — Gemini Pro, Ultra, Flash, Nano — with strong long-context and image/video/audio understanding.

title: Gemma (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Google's open-weights models (Gemma 1, 2, 3) derived from the same research as Gemini, sized from 1B to 27B with strong quality-per-parameter.

title: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer)
existing_short_definition: The decoder-only transformer architecture and OpenAI model family that established modern autoregressive LLMs as the dominant paradigm.

title: Granite (IBM Model Family)
existing_short_definition: IBM's open-weights LLMs designed for enterprise governance — known training data, clear provenance, and Apache 2.0 licensing.

title: Jamba (Hybrid Mamba-Transformer)
existing_short_definition: AI21 Labs' hybrid architecture interleaving Mamba and transformer attention layers, supporting 256k+ context with strong throughput.

title: Llama (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Meta's open-weights LLM family — Llama, Llama 2, Llama 3, Llama 4 — that defined the open-source frontier since 2023.

title: LLaVA
existing_short_definition: An open-source vision-language model family that glues a CLIP/SigLIP vision encoder onto an LLM via a lightweight projection layer, with instruction tuning on image-text pairs.

title: LLM (Large Language Model)
existing_short_definition: An AI that reads and generates human text — it's the brain behind ChatGPT, Claude, and most AI tools you've heard of.

title: Mamba (State Space Model)
existing_short_definition: An LLM architecture based on selective state-space models instead of attention, with linear-time inference and strong long-context performance.

title: Mistral (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Mistral AI's open-weights models — Mistral 7B, Mixtral 8x7B, Mixtral 8x22B, Mistral Large, Ministral — known for efficiency and MoE pioneering.

title: Mixtral
existing_short_definition: Mistral's mixture-of-experts models — 8×7B and 8×22B — with total parameters of 46B and 141B but only ~13B and ~39B active per token.

title: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
existing_short_definition: An architecture that activates only a small subset of the model's total parameters per token, giving you a big model's quality at a small model's compute cost.

title: Nomic Embed
existing_short_definition: Nomic AI's open-weights text embedding models with fully reproducible training (open data + open code) and strong MTEB performance.

title: OpenAI o1 / o3 (Reasoning Models)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's first public reasoning models (o1 series, late 2024; o3 series, 2025) that trade extra inference-time 'thinking' for sharply better performance on hard tasks.

title: Parameters (Model Size)
existing_short_definition: The numbers inside an AI model that were learned during training — more parameters generally means smarter but slower.

title: Phi (Small Language Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Microsoft's small language models — Phi-2, Phi-3, Phi-4 — demonstrating that carefully curated 'textbook-quality' data can make sub-10B models competitive with much larger ones.

title: Qwen (Model Family)
existing_short_definition: Alibaba's open-weights LLM family — Qwen, Qwen2, Qwen2.5, Qwen3 — strong on multilingual tasks, code, math, and agentic capabilities.

title: Qwen Coder
existing_short_definition: Alibaba's code-specialist variant of Qwen, strong on repo-level context, agentic code tasks, and multilingual programming.

title: Reasoning Model (Category)
existing_short_definition: An LLM specifically trained (usually via RL) to produce extended internal reasoning before answering, excelling at math, coding, and scientific problems.

title: RoBERTa
existing_short_definition: A robustly optimized BERT — same architecture, different pretraining recipe — that dropped NSP, trained longer on more data, and consistently outperforms BERT on classification tasks.

title: RWKV
existing_short_definition: A linear-attention RNN-transformer hybrid that trains like a transformer but runs inference like an RNN, with linear-time generation.

title: SAM (Segment Anything Model)
existing_short_definition: Meta's foundation model for promptable image segmentation — given a point, box, or mask, it produces a precise object mask zero-shot.

title: SigLIP
existing_short_definition: Google's CLIP successor that replaces the softmax contrastive loss with a sigmoid loss, improving scaling and quality especially for smaller batches.

title: Sora (Video Generation)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's text-to-video diffusion-transformer model (announced Feb 2024, public 2024), generating coherent multi-second HD video from text prompts.

title: Stable Diffusion
existing_short_definition: Stability AI's open-weights latent-diffusion text-to-image model family (SD 1.5, SDXL, SD3) that democratized generative imagery.

title: StarCoder (Code Model)
existing_short_definition: BigCode's open-source code LLM family trained on permissively licensed GitHub data, with strong code completion and repo-level context.

title: State Space Model
existing_short_definition: A sequence-model class that processes tokens through a linear recurrence with a learned hidden state, enabling linear-time inference.

title: T5 (Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer)
existing_short_definition: Google's encoder-decoder model that frames every NLP task — classification, translation, summarization — as text-to-text generation.

title: Transformer
existing_short_definition: The neural network architecture that powers almost every modern AI model — it's the 'T' in GPT and BERT.

title: Veo (Google Video Model)
existing_short_definition: Google DeepMind's text-to-video generation model (Veo 1, Veo 2, Veo 3) — strong on cinematic quality, motion, and prompt adherence.

title: Vision Transformer (ViT)
existing_short_definition: A transformer architecture that treats image patches as tokens, replacing CNNs as the dominant backbone for large-scale vision models.

title: Whisper (Speech Recognition)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's open-weight speech-to-text model family, trained on 680k hours of multilingual audio, with strong robustness to noise and accents.

title: YOLO (You Only Look Once)
existing_short_definition: A family of real-time object detection models — YOLOv1 through YOLO11 — that perform detection in a single forward pass for high FPS inference.
